nodes.name: Target
nodes.body: The molecular entity whose modulation is hypothesized to produce a therapeutic effect. Validated by genetic evidence, expression data, and functional assays.

nodes.name: Mechanism of Action
nodes.description: How the drug modulates the target
nodes.body: The biochemical interaction through which a drug produces its pharmacological effect. Examples: enzyme inhibition, receptor agonism/antagonism, protein degradation.

nodes.name: Biomarker
nodes.description: Measurable indicator of drug activity or patient selection
nodes.body: Used for patient stratification, pharmacodynamic monitoring, and efficacy endpoints. Types: predictive (selects patients), prognostic (disease outcome), pharmacodynamic (drug activity).

nodes.name: Hit Compound
nodes.body: Identified via high-throughput screening (HTS), fragment-based drug design, or virtual screening. Typically micromolar potency. Must be validated and confirmed as a starting point for optimization.

nodes.name: Lead Compound
nodes.body: Hit compound optimized for potency, selectivity, ADMET properties, and synthetic accessibility. Passes Lipinski Rule of Five criteria. Ready for preclinical evaluation.

nodes.name: Preclinical
nodes.description: In vitro and in vivo studies before human trials
nodes.body: Includes pharmacokinetics, toxicology (acute and chronic), safety pharmacology, and efficacy in animal models. Required for IND filing. Typical duration: 1-2 years.

nodes.name: Phase I Trial
nodes.body: 20-100 healthy volunteers or patients. Primary: safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics. Dose escalation (3+3 or Bayesian designs). Determines MTD and recommended Phase II dose. Duration: 6-12 months.

nodes.name: Phase II Trial
nodes.description: Proof of concept — efficacy and dose optimization
nodes.body: 100-300 patients. Primary: efficacy signal, dose-response. Randomized, often placebo-controlled. Go/no-go decision point. Highest attrition rate in development. Duration: 1-2 years.

nodes.name: Phase III Trial
nodes.body: 1000-5000+ patients. Randomized, double-blind, controlled. Primary endpoint must be clinically meaningful. Required for NDA/BLA submission. Duration: 2-4 years. Cost: $50M-$300M+.

nodes.name: Regulatory Approval
nodes.body: NDA (small molecules) or BLA (biologics) submission. Standard review: 10-12 months. Priority review: 6-8 months. Accelerated approval possible with surrogate endpoints. Post-marketing commitments required.

nodes.name: Adverse Event
nodes.description: Unwanted drug effect that may halt development
nodes.body: On-target (mechanism-related) or off-target toxicity. Dose-limiting toxicities set therapeutic window. Hepatotoxicity, cardiotoxicity, and neurotoxicity are common development-ending events.

nodes.name: Attrition / Failure
nodes.description: Program termination due to insufficient efficacy or safety
nodes.body: Overall success rate from Phase I to approval: ~10%. Top reasons: lack of efficacy (40-50%), safety/toxicity (30%), commercial viability (10-20%). Phase II has highest failure rate.
